title: Internal error on incorrect format string (%d instead of %s)
body:
The following code is clearly wrong (though it took me boiling it down to this short example to figure that out), but the internal error ain't great.

```sv
module crash
#(
  parameter string ENDPOINT_ID = ""
)
(
  input  logic clk
);
  always @(posedge clk) begin
          $error("%d", ENDPOINT_ID);
  end
endmodule
```

```
$ verilator --top-module crash --cc verilator_crash.sv
%Error: Internal Error: ../V3Number.h:206: `num` member accessed when data type is STRING
                        ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html for more assistance.
```

Verilator 5.026 on Ubuntu 20.04.
